document_texts.f6.txt:
Climate change represents the most urgent and devastating challenge facing humanity in the twenty-first century. The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change, commonly known as the IPCC, has issued stark warnings that global average temperatures have already risen by approximately 1.1 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels. Scientists at NASA and NOAA have confirmed through decades of careful measurement that this warming is primarily driven by the burning of fossil fuels and the resulting accumulation of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases in the atmosphere.

Greta Thunberg, the Swedish environmental activist, began her school strike for climate in August 2018 outside the Swedish parliament, inspiring millions of young people around the world to demand urgent action from their governments. The Fridays for Future movement that grew from her individual protest has organized some of the largest climate demonstrations in history. Thunberg has met with world leaders including Pope Francis, Barack Obama, and various heads of state, delivering an uncompromising message that current climate policies are dangerously inadequate.

The Paris Agreement, adopted in 2015 under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, committed participating nations to limiting global warming to well below 2 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels, with efforts to limit warming to 1.5 degrees. Despite this agreement, global carbon emissions have continued to rise in most years since its adoption. The United States withdrew from the Paris Agreement under President Donald Trump and subsequently rejoined under President Joe Biden, highlighting the political vulnerability of international climate commitments.

Amazon deforestation has accelerated alarmingly in recent years, with the Brazilian Amazon losing record areas of forest cover under policies that prioritized agricultural and mining development over environmental protection. Scientists warn that continued deforestation could push the Amazon past a tipping point beyond which large portions of the rainforest would transition to savanna, releasing enormous quantities of stored carbon and dramatically reducing biodiversity. The Amazon River basin contains approximately ten percent of all species on Earth and plays a crucial role in regulating South American and global climate patterns.

Renewable energy technology has advanced dramatically and costs have fallen to the point where solar and wind power are now the cheapest sources of new electricity generation in most parts of the world. Companies like Vestas in wind energy and First Solar and SunPower in solar energy have played leading roles in this transformation. Tesla's energy storage products and other battery technologies are enabling greater integration of renewable energy into electrical grids that previously required fossil fuel backup generation for reliability.

The consequences of inaction on climate change are catastrophic and irreversible on human timescales. Extreme weather events including hurricanes, floods, droughts, and wildfires are already becoming more frequent and severe. Coastal communities around the world face existential threats from sea level rise. The time for incremental action has passed and only transformative systemic change can prevent the worst outcomes.
document_texts.f7.txt: Climate change is one of the most serious challenges facing humanity in the twenty-first century. The United Nations Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change has issued alarming scientific reports confirming that global temperatures have risen by 1.1 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels. Major industrialized countries such as the United States of America, China, and the European Union bear the greatest historical responsibility for this rise as a result of burning fossil fuels for many decades. The Paris Climate Agreement, signed by one hundred and eighty-nine countries in 2015, aims to limit temperature rises to less than two degrees Celsius. Greta Thunberg, the young Swedish activist, launched the school strike movement for climate and inspired millions of young people around the world to demand climate justice. Thunberg met world leaders at the United Nations and in front of European parliaments, bringing a clear message that time is running out and future generations will not forgive inaction. Deforestation in the Brazilian Amazon has reached record levels of serious scientific concern. The Amazon rainforest contains ten percent of all living organisms on the planet and plays a pivotal role in regulating the global climate. Scientists warn that continued destruction could push this vital ecosystem towards a point of no return, where dense forests turn into dry savannah. Renewable energy has made amazing progress, with solar and wind energy becoming the lowest costs in human history. Companies like Vestas Wind and First Solar are leaders in this major energy transition. Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates are investing heavily in solar energy despite their historical dependence on oil and gas. Climate disasters are on the rise at an alarming rate in various parts of the world. The catastrophic floods in Pakistan in 2022 affected thirty million people and proved that the countries of the Global South suffer the most severe consequences, even though they are historically the least responsible for climate change. Climate justice requires that rich countries pay for the damage they inflict on developing countries. The future depends on political will, international cooperation and a rapid transition towards a zero-carbon economy. Every delay in taking the necessary action increases the human and economic costs and makes solutions more difficult and complex.
document_texts.f8.txt:
காலநிலை மாற்றம் இருபத்தியொன்றாம் நூற்றாண்டில் மனிதகுலம் எதிர்கொள்ளும் மிக முக்கியமான சவாலாக மாறியுள்ளது. ஐக்கிய நாடுகள் சபையின் காலநிலை மாற்றம் தொடர்பான அரசிடை குழு வெளியிட்ட அறிக்கைகள் உலக வெப்பநிலை தொழில் புரட்சிக்கு முந்தைய காலத்தை விட 1.1 டிகிரி செல்சியஸ் அதிகரித்துள்ளது என்பதை உறுதிப்படுத்துகின்றன. நாசா மற்றும் நோவா ஆய்வு நிறுவனங்கள் இந்த வெப்பமயமாதலுக்கு எரிபொருள் எரிப்பே முக்கிய காரணம் என்று நிரூபித்துள்ளன.

சுவீடிஷ் இளம் சுற்றுச்சூழல் ஆர்வலர் கிரேட்டா தன்பெர்க் 2018 ஆகஸ்ட் மாதம் சுவீடன் பாராளுமன்றத்திற்கு வெளியே பள்ளிப் புரட்டால் தொடங்கினார். இந்த ஒற்றை நபர் போராட்டம் உலகம் முழுவதும் பல மில்லியன் இளைஞர்களை ஈர்த்தது. தன்பெர்க் பாப் பிரான்சிஸ், பராக் ஒபாமா மற்றும் பல உலகத் தலைவர்களைச் சந்தித்து காலநிலை நடவடிக்கைகள் போதுமானதாக இல்லை என்று கடுமையாக எச்சரித்தார். பாரிஸ் ஒப்பந்தம் 2015 இல் கையெழுத்தானது இருந்தும் உலக கார்பன் வெளியீடுகள் தொடர்ந்து அதிகரிக்கின்றன.

அமேசான் காடுகள் அழிவு மிகவும் கவலைப்பட வேண்டிய நிலையில் உள்ளது. பிரேசிலின் அமேசான் பகுதியில் கோடிக்கணக்கான மரங்கள் வெட்டப்படுகின்றன. விஞ்ஞானிகள் எச்சரிக்கின்றனர் இந்த காடுகள் ஒரு திரும்பும் புள்ளியை தாண்டினால் சவன்னாவாக மாறி மிகப்பெரிய அளவிலான கார்பனை வெளியிடும். அமேசான் நதி படுகை பூமியிலுள்ள அனைத்து உயிரினங்களில் பத்து சதவீதத்தை உள்ளடக்கியது என்பது இதன் முக்கியத்துவத்தை காட்டுகிறது.

புதுப்பிக்கத்தக்க ஆற்றல் தொழில்நுட்பம் வியத்தகு முன்னேற்றம் கண்டுள்ளது. சூரிய சக்தி மற்றும் காற்றாலை மின்சாரம் இப்போது உலகின் பெரும்பாலான இடங்களில் மலிவான மின்சார ஆதாரங்களாக மாறியுள்ளன. வெஸ்டாஸ் நிறுவனம் காற்றாலை ஆற்றலிலும் ஃபர்ஸ்ட் சோலார் நிறுவனம் சூரிய ஆற்றலிலும் முன்னோடியாக செயல்படுகின்றன. டெஸ்லா நிறுவனம் மின்கார உற்பத்தியுடன் ஆற்றல் சேமிப்பு தீர்வுகளையும் வழங்குகிறது.

தமிழ்நாட்டில் காலநிலை மாற்றத்தின் விளைவுகள் மிகவும் தெளிவாக தெரிகின்றன. வடகிழக்கு பருவமழை ஒழுங்கற்றதாகிவிட்டது மற்றும் வெப்பமான கோடைக்காலங்கள் அதிகமாகியுள்ளன. சென்னை நகரம் கடல் மட்ட உயர்வினால் முன்னெப்போதும் இல்லாத வெள்ள ஆபத்தை எதிர்கொள்கிறது. விவசாயிகள் மாறும் மழைநீர் வடிவங்களால் கடுமையான நெருக்கடியில் உள்ளனர்.

உலகெங்கும் உள்ள இளைஞர்கள் காலநிலை நடவடிக்கைக்கு குரல் கொடுக்கின்றனர். அரசியல் இச்சாசக்தியும் சர்வதேச ஒத்துழைப்பும் மட்டுமே இந்த நெருக்கடியை தீர்க்க முடியும்.
document_texts.f9.txt: Climate change is the greatest crisis facing our generation. The United Nations' Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change has stated that global temperatures have risen by 1.1 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels. Decades of research by NASA and NOAA have proven that this warming is primarily caused by the burning of fossil fuels. The effects of this rising temperature are being felt all over the world. Greta Thunberg, a young Swedish environmental activist, started a climate walkout in front of the Swedish Parliament in 2018. This single man's movement inspired millions of youth around the world. Thunberg met with Pope Francis, Barack Obama and several world leaders to demand climate action. Although the Paris Agreement was signed in 2015, global carbon emissions continue to rise. Amazon deforestation is occurring at an alarming rate. Brazil's Amazon region is experiencing massive deforestation. Scientists have warned that if the deforestation is not stopped, the Amazon will pass a point of no return and dense forests will turn into dry grasslands. Ten percent of all species on Earth are found in the Amazon River Basin. Renewable energy technology is developing very rapidly. Solar energy and wind energy have now become the cheapest energy sources in most parts of the world. Vestas, First Solar and SunPower are leading companies in this field. Tesla's energy storage products have revolutionized the use of renewable energy. Solar power projects are expanding rapidly in Maharashtra. The effects of climate change are now clearly visible in Maharashtra. Drought has increased in Marathwada while extreme rainfall events are happening frequently in Konkan. It is a very difficult time for farmers as the rain patterns are changing making it difficult to plan crops. Even in cities like Pune, the frequency of heat waves is increasing. Urgent and drastic measures are needed to fight climate change. It is our responsibility to leave a healthy and beautiful earth for future generations.
document_texts.f10.txt:
The COVID-19 pandemic, caused by the SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus, represents the most significant global health crisis since the influenza pandemic of 1918. The World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a public health emergency of international concern in January 2020 and a pandemic in March 2020. The virus first emerged in Wuhan, China in late 2019 and rapidly spread to every country on Earth, killing millions of people and causing unprecedented economic disruption. The pandemic exposed deep vulnerabilities in global health systems and supply chains that had been building for decades.

Pfizer and BioNTech developed the first mRNA vaccine against COVID-19, with Pfizer's CEO Albert Bourla announcing remarkable efficacy results in November 2020. The vaccine was authorized for emergency use by regulatory agencies including the FDA in the United States and the EMA in Europe within months of the pandemic being declared. Moderna, another biotechnology company, developed a second highly effective mRNA vaccine using similar technology. The speed at which these vaccines were developed was unprecedented in the history of medicine, made possible by decades of prior research into mRNA technology and enormous government investment.

The development and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines represented one of the greatest achievements in the history of public health. However, vaccine inequity became a major ethical and practical problem, with wealthy nations hoarding vaccine supplies while low-income countries struggled to vaccinate their populations. The COVAX initiative, led by the WHO, Gavi the Vaccine Alliance, and the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations, worked to improve equitable distribution but faced enormous logistical and funding challenges.

Anthony Fauci, who served as director of the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases for decades, became the most prominent public health official in the United States during the pandemic. Fauci provided clear scientific guidance during periods of enormous uncertainty and faced intense political pressure that he consistently resisted in favor of evidence-based recommendations. His relationship with the Trump administration became strained as the administration sometimes contradicted scientific consensus on issues including mask wearing and treatment options.

Long COVID, a condition in which patients experience persistent symptoms for months or years after acute infection, emerged as a significant public health concern affecting millions of people worldwide. Symptoms include severe fatigue, brain fog, breathing difficulties, and cardiovascular problems that can be debilitating. Research institutions including the National Institutes of Health launched major studies to understand the mechanisms of long COVID and develop effective treatments. The condition highlighted the importance of taking viral infections seriously even when initial symptoms appear mild.

The pandemic accelerated the development of digital health technologies including telemedicine platforms, remote patient monitoring systems, and AI-powered diagnostic tools. Companies like Teladoc, Amwell, and Epic Systems saw dramatic increases in adoption of their platforms. The integration of these technologies into mainstream healthcare has permanently changed how medicine is practiced in many countries.
document_texts.f11.txt: The COVID-19 pandemic has put global health systems to an unprecedented test. The novel coronavirus SARS-CoV-2 spread from Wuhan in China to all countries in the world within a few months and claimed millions of lives. The World Health Organization, under its Director General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, declared a global health emergency in March 2020. Older people and people with previous illnesses such as diabetes, heart disease and respiratory diseases were particularly affected. Pfizer and BioNTech developed the world's first mRNA vaccine against COVID-19 in record time. Albert Bourla, the CEO of Pfizer, announced the exceptionally high effectiveness of the vaccine in November 2020. BioNTech, a German biotechnology company, had been researching mRNA technology for many years and was now able to use this expertise in the crisis. The European Medicines Agency and the FDA in the US granted emergency approval within a few months. Moderna also developed an mRNA vaccine with comparable effectiveness. The rapid development of both vaccines was only possible through decades of basic research and massive government investments. Germany invested significant resources in vaccine development through BioNTech, contributing to one of the most significant medical achievements in history. The federal government under Chancellor Angela Merkel coordinated European vaccine procurement and national vaccination campaigns. Long COVID as a secondary disease affects millions of people worldwide and presents health systems with new challenges. Those affected suffer from persistent fatigue, problems concentrating and shortness of breath, which can persist for months or years after the acute infection. German research institutions including the Charité in Berlin and the German Center for Infection Research are working intensively on research into this disease. The social and economic costs of Long COVID are enormous. The pandemic has significantly accelerated the digitalization of the German healthcare system. Telemedicine platforms boomed and were used by millions of patients for the first time. Companies such as Techniker Krankenkasse and Gematik are working to sustainably modernize the digital infrastructure of the healthcare system. Artificial intelligence is increasingly being used for the early detection of diseases and the development of new drugs. The lessons from the pandemic must be incorporated into a better global health architecture. Pandemic prevention, vaccine equity and the strengthening of international health institutions are the tasks of the coming decades.

This virus has killed millions of people and caused huge damage to the global economy. Pfizer and BioNTech together developed the world's first mRNA vaccine, one of the greatest achievements in medical science. Pfizer CEO Albert Bourla announced the extraordinary effectiveness of the vaccine in November 2020. The US FDA and Europe's EMA granted emergency use authorization within a few months. Moderna also developed a highly effective vaccine using the same technology. Anthony Fauci, who for decades was the director of the US National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, became the most important public health official during the pandemic. The COVAX initiative was led by WHO, Gavi and the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations, working to deliver vaccines to poor countries. document_texts.f14.txt:
The FIFA World Cup 2022 held in Qatar was one of the most dramatic and controversial tournaments in the history of international football. Lionel Messi, widely considered the greatest footballer of his generation, finally led Argentina to World Cup glory in a final against France that many observers described as the greatest World Cup final ever played. The match ended 3-3 after extra time with Messi scoring twice, before Argentina won on penalties. For Messi, who had long lived in the shadow of his predecessor Diego Maradona in terms of World Cup achievement, the victory was a culmination of decades of dedication and brilliance.

Kylian Mbappe of France delivered one of the most astonishing individual performances in World Cup final history, scoring a hat-trick including two goals in rapid succession that drew France level from 2-0 down. Despite his heroics France could not prevent Argentina from winning their third World Cup title. Mbappe, who was just twenty-three years old at the time of the final, plays for Paris Saint-Germain in Ligue 1 and is widely expected to become the defining footballer of the next decade. Real Madrid has been linked with a record-breaking transfer for Mbappe for several years.

The esports industry has grown into a multibillion-dollar global phenomenon with professional tournaments attracting audiences that rival traditional sports. Riot Games developed League of Legends, which has become one of the most watched esports globally with major tournaments filling football stadiums across Asia and North America. Valve Corporation's Dota 2 features The International tournament with prize pools that have reached over forty million dollars, funded through a community crowdfunding model that has become a template for the industry.

Fortnite, developed by Epic Games, transformed the gaming industry with its free-to-play battle royale model and became a cultural phenomenon that transcended gaming. At its peak Fortnite had over three hundred and fifty million registered accounts and hosted virtual concerts featuring artists including Travis Scott and Ariana Grande that drew tens of millions of concurrent viewers. Epic Games founder Tim Sweeney has been a vocal critic of Apple's App Store policies and led a legal battle against Apple that became a landmark case in digital marketplace regulation.

The NBA continues to be one of the most globally popular sports leagues in the world. LeBron James of the Los Angeles Lakers has become the all-time leading scorer in NBA history, surpassing the record previously held by Kareem Abdul-Jabbar. Stephen Curry of the Golden State Warriors revolutionized basketball with his extraordinary three-point shooting ability, inspiring a generation of players worldwide to develop long-range shooting skills. The NBA's partnership with the Chinese market has been complicated by political tensions but remains strategically important.

The intersection of technology and sports continues to deepen through applications including advanced analytics, computer vision-based performance tracking, virtual reality training environments, and AI-powered injury prevention systems. Major League Baseball adopted the automated ball-strike system in minor league games to improve officiating consistency and is considering expanding it to the major leagues.

document_texts.f18.txt:
SpaceX, founded by Elon Musk in 2002, has fundamentally transformed the space industry through the development of reusable rocket technology and a relentless focus on cost reduction. The company's Falcon 9 rocket, which can land and be relaunched multiple times, has dramatically reduced the cost of putting satellites into orbit. SpaceX has launched thousands of Starlink internet satellites forming a constellation that provides broadband internet access to remote and underserved areas around the world. The company's Dragon spacecraft has restored American human spaceflight capability after the retirement of the Space Shuttle in 2011.

NASA's Artemis program aims to return humans to the Moon by the mid-2020s with the goal of establishing a sustainable long-term presence on the lunar surface. SpaceX was selected by NASA to develop the Human Landing System for the Artemis program, beating out Blue Origin founded by Jeff Bezos and Dynetics in a competitive selection process. Bill Nelson, who serves as NASA Administrator, has emphasized that the Artemis program represents a new era of lunar exploration that will include the first woman and first person of color to walk on the Moon.

The James Webb Space Telescope, launched on Christmas Day 2021, has delivered breathtaking images and scientific data that have transformed our understanding of the early universe. The telescope, developed jointly by NASA, the European Space Agency, and the Canadian Space Agency, is capable of observing galaxies that formed just a few hundred million years after the Big Bang. The images released by NASA showing distant galaxies, star-forming nebulae, and the atmospheres of exoplanets generated widespread public excitement about space exploration and astronomy.

India's space program achieved a historic milestone in August 2023 when the Chandrayaan-3 mission successfully landed a rover near the lunar south pole. The Indian Space Research Organisation became only the fourth space agency to achieve a soft landing on the Moon and the first to do so near the south pole, which is of great scientific interest because of evidence of water ice deposits. Prime Minister Narendra Modi celebrated the achievement alongside ISRO scientists as a testament to India's growing technological capabilities.

The search for extraterrestrial life has intensified with the discovery of thousands of exoplanets using the Kepler Space Telescope and the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite. Scientists at institutions including the SETI Institute and NASA's Astrobiology program are developing new methods to detect biosignatures in the atmospheres of distant planets. The discovery of phosphine gas in the atmosphere of Venus, announced in 2020, sparked significant scientific debate about whether it could indicate the presence of microbial life in the Venusian cloud layer.

The commercialization of space is accelerating rapidly with companies including Blue Origin, Rocket Lab, Virgin Galactic, and numerous others developing launch vehicles and space tourism capabilities. The International Space Station, operated jointly by NASA, Roscosmos, ESA, JAXA, and CSA, continues to serve as a platform for scientific research in microgravity conditions. Decisions about the future of the space station after 2030 will shape the trajectory of international cooperation in space for decades.

document_texts.f20.txt: SpaceX was founded in 2002 by Elon Musk and the company has revolutionized the space industry by developing reusable rocket technology. Falcon 9 rocket can land and take off again, which has significantly reduced the cost of satellite launch. NASA's Artemis program aims to send humans back to the Moon by the mid-2020s. SpaceX was given the contract by NASA to develop Human Landing System in which Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin company was defeated. The James Webb Space Telescope was launched on 25 December 2021 and has returned stunning images of the early universe. NASA, European Space Agency and Canadian Space Agency have jointly developed this telescope. This telescope can see galaxies formed just a few hundred million years after the Big Bang, giving a new dimension to the understanding of our universe. India's space program achieved a historic feat in August 2023 when Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed near the south pole of the Moon. Indian Space Research Organization ISRO became the fourth country to perform a soft landing on the Moon. Prime Minister Narendra Modi celebrated this historic achievement with ISRO scientists. This success is a testament to India's growing technological capabilities. Thousands of exoplanets have been discovered with the help of Kepler Space Telescope and Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite. The SETI Institute and NASA's Astrobiology Program are developing new methods to search for signs of life in the atmospheres of distant planets. The discovery of phosphine gas in the atmosphere of Venus in 2020 sparked new debate on the possibility of extraterrestrial life. Companies like Blue Origin, Rocket Lab and Virgin Galactic are working towards making space tourism a reality. The International Space Station is operated by a joint effort of NASA, Roscosmos, ESA, JAXA and CSA. NVIDIA's GPU chips and artificial intelligence are now being used to analyze space data and process satellite images. Space exploration is humanity's most ambitious journey to help us understand our place in the universe. Cooperation in space can become a powerful means of peace and scientific advancement among countries.
document_texts.f1.txt:
OpenAI was founded in 2015 by Sam Altman, Elon Musk, Greg Brockman, and several other prominent researchers in the field of artificial intelligence. The organization was initially established as a non-profit research laboratory with the primary goal of ensuring that artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity. Over the years, OpenAI has developed some of the most transformative technologies in the history of computing, including the GPT series of large language models.

Sam Altman serves as the Chief Executive Officer of OpenAI and has been instrumental in guiding the company toward commercial viability while maintaining its research-focused mission. Under his leadership, OpenAI launched ChatGPT in November 2022, which rapidly became one of the fastest-growing consumer applications in history, reaching one hundred million users within just two months of its release. This achievement demonstrated the massive public appetite for accessible artificial intelligence tools.

GPT-4, released by OpenAI in March 2023, represented a significant leap forward in language model capabilities. The model demonstrated remarkable performance across a wide range of professional and academic benchmarks, including passing the bar exam and the medical licensing examination at scores that surpassed the majority of human test-takers. Microsoft invested heavily in OpenAI, committing ten billion dollars to the partnership, and integrated GPT-4 into products including Bing, Azure, and Microsoft Office applications.

Google responded to the success of ChatGPT by accelerating development of its own large language model, Gemini, which was developed by Google DeepMind. DeepMind itself was originally founded in London in 2010 and was acquired by Google in 2014 for approximately five hundred million dollars. The competition between OpenAI and Google has driven rapid advancement in the field, benefiting researchers, developers, and end users worldwide.

Anthropic, founded by Dario Amodei and Daniela Amodei after they departed from OpenAI, developed Claude as a competing large language model focused on safety and constitutional AI principles. The company has raised substantial funding from investors including Google and Spark Capital. The emergence of multiple well-funded AI laboratories has created a highly competitive landscape that is accelerating the pace of innovation across the entire technology industry.

The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence technology has raised important questions about regulation, safety, and the long-term impact on employment and society. Organizations like the Partnership on AI and various government bodies around the world are working to develop frameworks that can guide responsible development and deployment of these powerful systems.
document_texts.f2.txt:
Tesla was founded in 2003 by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning, though Elon Musk joined as chairman of the board in 2004 and later became CEO. Tesla designs and manufactures electric vehicles, battery energy storage systems, and solar energy products. The company has fundamentally disrupted the global automotive industry by proving that electric vehicles can be both desirable and commercially successful at scale.

Elon Musk has been the driving force behind Tesla's aggressive expansion strategy, pushing the company to build Gigafactories across the United States, Germany, and China. The Shanghai Gigafactory, which opened in 2019, dramatically increased Tesla's production capacity and allowed the company to serve the enormous Chinese electric vehicle market more competitively. Tesla's Autopilot and Full Self-Driving software systems represent some of the most advanced applications of neural networks and computer vision in the automotive sector.

NVIDIA has emerged as the dominant supplier of graphics processing units used for training large-scale artificial intelligence models. Jensen Huang, who co-founded NVIDIA in 1993, has positioned the company as the essential infrastructure provider for the artificial intelligence revolution. NVIDIA's H100 GPU chips are in extraordinary demand from technology companies, cloud providers, and research institutions around the world. The company's market capitalization surpassed two trillion dollars in 2024, reflecting its central role in enabling modern artificial intelligence workloads.

The relationship between NVIDIA and major cloud providers including Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud has become increasingly important as demand for AI computing resources continues to grow exponentially. These cloud providers are also developing their own custom silicon chips to reduce dependency on NVIDIA and lower the cost of running AI inference workloads at scale. Amazon developed the Trainium and Inferentia chips for this purpose, while Google created the Tensor Processing Unit.

Deep learning frameworks developed by Google, including TensorFlow, and by Facebook, including PyTorch, have become the standard tools used by researchers and engineers worldwide to build and train neural networks. The open-source nature of these frameworks has accelerated progress across the entire field by allowing anyone with sufficient computing resources to experiment with state-of-the-art techniques. The collaborative culture of sharing research through platforms like arXiv has been essential to the rapid pace of advancement in artificial intelligence.

The integration of artificial intelligence into every major technology product represents both an extraordinary opportunity and a significant responsibility. Companies that successfully harness these capabilities will likely achieve substantial competitive advantages, while those that fail to adapt may struggle to remain relevant in an increasingly AI-driven economy.

document_texts.f6.txt:
Climate change represents the most urgent and devastating challenge facing humanity in the twenty-first century. The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change, commonly known as the IPCC, has issued stark warnings that global average temperatures have already risen by approximately 1.1 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels. Scientists at NASA and NOAA have confirmed through decades of careful measurement that this warming is primarily driven by the burning of fossil fuels and the resulting accumulation of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases in the atmosphere.

Greta Thunberg, the Swedish environmental activist, began her school strike for climate in August 2018 outside the Swedish parliament, inspiring millions of young people around the world to demand urgent action from their governments. The Fridays for Future movement that grew from her individual protest has organized some of the largest climate demonstrations in history. Thunberg has met with world leaders including Pope Francis, Barack Obama, and various heads of state, delivering an uncompromising message that current climate policies are dangerously inadequate.

The Paris Agreement, adopted in 2015 under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, committed participating nations to limiting global warming to well below 2 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels, with efforts to limit warming to 1.5 degrees. Despite this agreement, global carbon emissions have continued to rise in most years since its adoption. The United States withdrew from the Paris Agreement under President Donald Trump and subsequently rejoined under President Joe Biden, highlighting the political vulnerability of international climate commitments.

Amazon deforestation has accelerated alarmingly in recent years, with the Brazilian Amazon losing record areas of forest cover under policies that prioritized agricultural and mining development over environmental protection. Scientists warn that continued deforestation could push the Amazon past a tipping point beyond which large portions of the rainforest would transition to savanna, releasing enormous quantities of stored carbon and dramatically reducing biodiversity. The Amazon River basin contains approximately ten percent of all species on Earth and plays a crucial role in regulating South American and global climate patterns.

Renewable energy technology has advanced dramatically and costs have fallen to the point where solar and wind power are now the cheapest sources of new electricity generation in most parts of the world. Companies like Vestas in wind energy and First Solar and SunPower in solar energy have played leading roles in this transformation. Tesla's energy storage products and other battery technologies are enabling greater integration of renewable energy into electrical grids that previously required fossil fuel backup generation for reliability.

The consequences of inaction on climate change are catastrophic and irreversible on human timescales. Extreme weather events including hurricanes, floods, droughts, and wildfires are already becoming more frequent and severe. Coastal communities around the world face existential threats from sea level rise. The time for incremental action has passed and only transformative systemic change can prevent the worst outcomes.
document_texts.f7.txt: Climate change is one of the most serious challenges facing humanity in the twenty-first century. The United Nations Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change has issued alarming scientific reports confirming that global temperatures have risen by 1.1 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels. Major industrialized countries such as the United States of America, China, and the European Union bear the greatest historical responsibility for this rise as a result of burning fossil fuels for many decades. The Paris Climate Agreement, signed by one hundred and eighty-nine countries in 2015, aims to limit temperature rises to less than two degrees Celsius. Greta Thunberg, the young Swedish activist, launched the school strike movement for climate and inspired millions of young people around the world to demand climate justice. Thunberg met world leaders at the United Nations and in front of European parliaments, bringing a clear message that time is running out and future generations will not forgive inaction. Deforestation in the Brazilian Amazon has reached record levels of serious scientific concern. The Amazon rainforest contains ten percent of all living organisms on the planet and plays a pivotal role in regulating the global climate. Scientists warn that continued destruction could push this vital ecosystem towards a point of no return, where dense forests turn into dry savannah. Renewable energy has made amazing progress, with solar and wind energy becoming the lowest costs in human history. Companies like Vestas Wind and First Solar are leaders in this major energy transition. Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates are investing heavily in solar energy despite their historical dependence on oil and gas. Climate disasters are on the rise at an alarming rate in various parts of the world. The catastrophic floods in Pakistan in 2022 affected thirty million people and proved that the countries of the Global South suffer the most severe consequences, even though they are historically the least responsible for climate change. Climate justice requires that rich countries pay for the damage they inflict on developing countries. The future depends on political will, international cooperation and a rapid transition towards a zero-carbon economy. Every delay in taking the necessary action increases the human and economic costs and makes solutions more difficult and complex.
document_texts.f8.txt:
காலநிலை மாற்றம் இருபத்தியொன்றாம் நூற்றாண்டில் மனிதகுலம் எதிர்கொள்ளும் மிக முக்கியமான சவாலாக மாறியுள்ளது. ஐக்கிய நாடுகள் சபையின் காலநிலை மாற்றம் தொடர்பான அரசிடை குழு வெளியிட்ட அறிக்கைகள் உலக வெப்பநிலை தொழில் புரட்சிக்கு முந்தைய காலத்தை விட 1.1 டிகிரி செல்சியஸ் அதிகரித்துள்ளது என்பதை உறுதிப்படுத்துகின்றன. நாசா மற்றும் நோவா ஆய்வு நிறுவனங்கள் இந்த வெப்பமயமாதலுக்கு எரிபொருள் எரிப்பே முக்கிய காரணம் என்று நிரூபித்துள்ளன.

சுவீடிஷ் இளம் சுற்றுச்சூழல் ஆர்வலர் கிரேட்டா தன்பெர்க் 2018 ஆகஸ்ட் மாதம் சுவீடன் பாராளுமன்றத்திற்கு வெளியே பள்ளிப் புரட்டால் தொடங்கினார். இந்த ஒற்றை நபர் போராட்டம் உலகம் முழுவதும் பல மில்லியன் இளைஞர்களை ஈர்த்தது. தன்பெர்க் பாப் பிரான்சிஸ், பராக் ஒபாமா மற்றும் பல உலகத் தலைவர்களைச் சந்தித்து காலநிலை நடவடிக்கைகள் போதுமானதாக இல்லை என்று கடுமையாக எச்சரித்தார். பாரிஸ் ஒப்பந்தம் 2015 இல் கையெழுத்தானது இருந்தும் உலக கார்பன் வெளியீடுகள் தொடர்ந்து அதிகரிக்கின்றன.

அமேசான் காடுகள் அழிவு மிகவும் கவலைப்பட வேண்டிய நிலையில் உள்ளது. பிரேசிலின் அமேசான் பகுதியில் கோடிக்கணக்கான மரங்கள் வெட்டப்படுகின்றன. விஞ்ஞானிகள் எச்சரிக்கின்றனர் இந்த காடுகள் ஒரு திரும்பும் புள்ளியை தாண்டினால் சவன்னாவாக மாறி மிகப்பெரிய அளவிலான கார்பனை வெளியிடும். அமேசான் நதி படுகை பூமியிலுள்ள அனைத்து உயிரினங்களில் பத்து சதவீதத்தை உள்ளடக்கியது என்பது இதன் முக்கியத்துவத்தை காட்டுகிறது.

புதுப்பிக்கத்தக்க ஆற்றல் தொழில்நுட்பம் வியத்தகு முன்னேற்றம் கண்டுள்ளது. சூரிய சக்தி மற்றும் காற்றாலை மின்சாரம் இப்போது உலகின் பெரும்பாலான இடங்களில் மலிவான மின்சார ஆதாரங்களாக மாறியுள்ளன. வெஸ்டாஸ் நிறுவனம் காற்றாலை ஆற்றலிலும் ஃபர்ஸ்ட் சோலார் நிறுவனம் சூரிய ஆற்றலிலும் முன்னோடியாக செயல்படுகின்றன. டெஸ்லா நிறுவனம் மின்கார உற்பத்தியுடன் ஆற்றல் சேமிப்பு தீர்வுகளையும் வழங்குகிறது.

தமிழ்நாட்டில் காலநிலை மாற்றத்தின் விளைவுகள் மிகவும் தெளிவாக தெரிகின்றன. வடகிழக்கு பருவமழை ஒழுங்கற்றதாகிவிட்டது மற்றும் வெப்பமான கோடைக்காலங்கள் அதிகமாகியுள்ளன. சென்னை நகரம் கடல் மட்ட உயர்வினால் முன்னெப்போதும் இல்லாத வெள்ள ஆபத்தை எதிர்கொள்கிறது. விவசாயிகள் மாறும் மழைநீர் வடிவங்களால் கடுமையான நெருக்கடியில் உள்ளனர்.

உலகெங்கும் உள்ள இளைஞர்கள் காலநிலை நடவடிக்கைக்கு குரல் கொடுக்கின்றனர். அரசியல் இச்சாசக்தியும் சர்வதேச ஒத்துழைப்பும் மட்டுமே இந்த நெருக்கடியை தீர்க்க முடியும்.

Urgent and drastic measures are needed to fight climate change. It is our responsibility to leave a healthy and beautiful earth for future generations.
document_texts.f10.txt:
The COVID-19 pandemic, caused by the SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus, represents the most significant global health crisis since the influenza pandemic of 1918. The World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a public health emergency of international concern in January 2020 and a pandemic in March 2020. The virus first emerged in Wuhan, China in late 2019 and rapidly spread to every country on Earth, killing millions of people and causing unprecedented economic disruption. The pandemic exposed deep vulnerabilities in global health systems and supply chains that had been building for decades.

Pfizer and BioNTech developed the first mRNA vaccine against COVID-19, with Pfizer's CEO Albert Bourla announcing remarkable efficacy results in November 2020. The vaccine was authorized for emergency use by regulatory agencies including the FDA in the United States and the EMA in Europe within months of the pandemic being declared. Moderna, another biotechnology company, developed a second highly effective mRNA vaccine using similar technology. The speed at which these vaccines were developed was unprecedented in the history of medicine, made possible by decades of prior research into mRNA technology and enormous government investment.

The development and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines represented one of the greatest achievements in the history of public health. However, vaccine inequity became a major ethical and practical problem, with wealthy nations hoarding vaccine supplies while low-income countries struggled to vaccinate their populations. The COVAX initiative, led by the WHO, Gavi the Vaccine Alliance, and the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations, worked to improve equitable distribution but faced enormous logistical and funding challenges.

Anthony Fauci, who served as director of the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases for decades, became the most prominent public health official in the United States during the pandemic. Fauci provided clear scientific guidance during periods of enormous uncertainty and faced intense political pressure that he consistently resisted in favor of evidence-based recommendations. His relationship with the Trump administration became strained as the administration sometimes contradicted scientific consensus on issues including mask wearing and treatment options.

Long COVID, a condition in which patients experience persistent symptoms for months or years after acute infection, emerged as a significant public health concern affecting millions of people worldwide. Symptoms include severe fatigue, brain fog, breathing difficulties, and cardiovascular problems that can be debilitating. Research institutions including the National Institutes of Health launched major studies to understand the mechanisms of long COVID and develop effective treatments. The condition highlighted the importance of taking viral infections seriously even when initial symptoms appear mild.

The pandemic accelerated the development of digital health technologies including telemedicine platforms, remote patient monitoring systems, and AI-powered diagnostic tools. Companies like Teladoc, Amwell, and Epic Systems saw dramatic increases in adoption of their platforms. The integration of these technologies into mainstream healthcare has permanently changed how medicine is practiced in many countries.

Artificial intelligence is increasingly being used for the early detection of diseases and the development of new drugs. The lessons from the pandemic must be incorporated into a better global health architecture. Pandemic prevention, vaccine equity and the strengthening of international health institutions are the tasks of the coming decades.

The FIFA World Cup 2022 held in Qatar was one of the most dramatic and controversial tournaments in the history of international football. Lionel Messi, widely considered the greatest footballer of his generation, finally led Argentina to World Cup glory in a final against France that many observers described as the greatest World Cup final ever played. The match ended 3-3 after extra time with Messi scoring twice, before Argentina won on penalties. For Messi, who had long lived in the shadow of his predecessor Diego Maradona in terms of World Cup achievement, the victory was a culmination of decades of dedication and brilliance.

Kylian Mbappe of France delivered one of the most astonishing individual performances in World Cup final history, scoring a hat-trick including two goals in rapid succession that drew France level from 2-0 down. Despite his heroics France could not prevent Argentina from winning their third World Cup title. Mbappe, who was just twenty-three years old at the time of the final, plays for Paris Saint-Germain in Ligue 1 and is widely expected to become the defining footballer of the next decade. Real Madrid has been linked with a record-breaking transfer for Mbappe for several years.

The esports industry has grown into a multibillion-dollar global phenomenon with professional tournaments attracting audiences that rival traditional sports. Riot Games developed League of Legends, which has become one of the most watched esports globally with major tournaments filling football stadiums across Asia and North America. Valve Corporation's Dota 2 features The International tournament with prize pools that have reached over forty million dollars, funded through a community crowdfunding model that has become a template for the industry.

Fortnite, developed by Epic Games, transformed the gaming industry with its free-to-play battle royale model and became a cultural phenomenon that transcended gaming. At its peak Fortnite had over three hundred and fifty million registered accounts and hosted virtual concerts featuring artists including Travis Scott and Ariana Grande that drew tens of millions of concurrent viewers. Epic Games founder Tim Sweeney has been a vocal critic of Apple's App Store policies and led a legal battle against Apple that became a landmark case in digital marketplace regulation.

The NBA continues to be one of the most globally popular sports leagues in the world. LeBron James of the Los Angeles Lakers has become the all-time leading scorer in NBA history, surpassing the record previously held by Kareem Abdul-Jabbar. Stephen Curry of the Golden State Warriors revolutionized basketball with his extraordinary three-point shooting ability, inspiring a generation of players worldwide to develop long-range shooting skills. The NBA's partnership with the Chinese market has been complicated by political tensions but remains strategically important.

The intersection of technology and sports continues to deepen through applications including advanced analytics, computer vision-based performance tracking, virtual reality training environments, and AI-powered injury prevention systems. Major League Baseball adopted the automated ball-strike system in minor league games to improve officiating consistency and is considering expanding it to the major leagues.

document_texts.f18.txt:
SpaceX, founded by Elon Musk in 2002, has fundamentally transformed the space industry through the development of reusable rocket technology and a relentless focus on cost reduction. The company's Falcon 9 rocket, which can land and be relaunched multiple times, has dramatically reduced the cost of putting satellites into orbit. SpaceX has launched thousands of Starlink internet satellites forming a constellation that provides broadband internet access to remote and underserved areas around the world. The company's Dragon spacecraft has restored American human spaceflight capability after the retirement of the Space Shuttle in 2011.

NASA's Artemis program aims to return humans to the Moon by the mid-2020s with the goal of establishing a sustainable long-term presence on the lunar surface. SpaceX was selected by NASA to develop the Human Landing System for the Artemis program, beating out Blue Origin founded by Jeff Bezos and Dynetics in a competitive selection process. Bill Nelson, who serves as NASA Administrator, has emphasized that the Artemis program represents a new era of lunar exploration that will include the first woman and first person of color to walk on the Moon.

The James Webb Space Telescope, launched on Christmas Day 2021, has delivered breathtaking images and scientific data that have transformed our understanding of the early universe. The telescope, developed jointly by NASA, the European Space Agency, and the Canadian Space Agency, is capable of observing galaxies that formed just a few hundred million years after the Big Bang. The images released by NASA showing distant galaxies, star-forming nebulae, and the atmospheres of exoplanets generated widespread public excitement about space exploration and astronomy.

India's space program achieved a historic milestone in August 2023 when the Chandrayaan-3 mission successfully landed a rover near the lunar south pole. The Indian Space Research Organisation became only the fourth space agency to achieve a soft landing on the Moon and the first to do so near the south pole, which is of great scientific interest because of evidence of water ice deposits. Prime Minister Narendra Modi celebrated the achievement alongside ISRO scientists as a testament to India's growing technological capabilities.

The search for extraterrestrial life has intensified with the discovery of thousands of exoplanets using the Kepler Space Telescope and the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite. Scientists at institutions including the SETI Institute and NASA's Astrobiology program are developing new methods to detect biosignatures in the atmospheres of distant planets. The discovery of phosphine gas in the atmosphere of Venus, announced in 2020, sparked significant scientific debate about whether it could indicate the presence of microbial life in the Venusian cloud layer.

The commercialization of space is accelerating rapidly with companies including Blue Origin, Rocket Lab, Virgin Galactic, and numerous others developing launch vehicles and space tourism capabilities. The International Space Station, operated jointly by NASA, Roscosmos, ESA, JAXA, and CSA, continues to serve as a platform for scientific research in microgravity conditions. Decisions about the future of the space station after 2030 will shape the trajectory of international cooperation in space for decades.

document_texts.f1.txt:
OpenAI was founded in 2015 by Sam Altman, Elon Musk, Greg Brockman, and several other prominent researchers in the field of artificial intelligence. The organization was initially established as a non-profit research laboratory with the primary goal of ensuring that artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity. Over the years, OpenAI has developed some of the most transformative technologies in the history of computing, including the GPT series of large language models.

Sam Altman serves as the Chief Executive Officer of OpenAI and has been instrumental in guiding the company toward commercial viability while maintaining its research-focused mission. Under his leadership, OpenAI launched ChatGPT in November 2022, which rapidly became one of the fastest-growing consumer applications in history, reaching one hundred million users within just two months of its release. This achievement demonstrated the massive public appetite for accessible artificial intelligence tools.

GPT-4, released by OpenAI in March 2023, represented a significant leap forward in language model capabilities. The model demonstrated remarkable performance across a wide range of professional and academic benchmarks, including passing the bar exam and the medical licensing examination at scores that surpassed the majority of human test-takers. Microsoft invested heavily in OpenAI, committing ten billion dollars to the partnership, and integrated GPT-4 into products including Bing, Azure, and Microsoft Office applications.

Google responded to the success of ChatGPT by accelerating development of its own large language model, Gemini, which was developed by Google DeepMind. DeepMind itself was originally founded in London in 2010 and was acquired by Google in 2014 for approximately five hundred million dollars. The competition between OpenAI and Google has driven rapid advancement in the field, benefiting researchers, developers, and end users worldwide.

Anthropic, founded by Dario Amodei and Daniela Amodei after they departed from OpenAI, developed Claude as a competing large language model focused on safety and constitutional AI principles. The company has raised substantial funding from investors including Google and Spark Capital. The emergence of multiple well-funded AI laboratories has created a highly competitive landscape that is accelerating the pace of innovation across the entire technology industry.

The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence technology has raised important questions about regulation, safety, and the long-term impact on employment and society. Organizations like the Partnership on AI and various government bodies around the world are working to develop frameworks that can guide responsible development and deployment of these powerful systems.
document_texts.f2.txt:
Tesla was founded in 2003 by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning, though Elon Musk joined as chairman of the board in 2004 and later became CEO. Tesla designs and manufactures electric vehicles, battery energy storage systems, and solar energy products. The company has fundamentally disrupted the global automotive industry by proving that electric vehicles can be both desirable and commercially successful at scale.

Elon Musk has been the driving force behind Tesla's aggressive expansion strategy, pushing the company to build Gigafactories across the United States, Germany, and China. The Shanghai Gigafactory, which opened in 2019, dramatically increased Tesla's production capacity and allowed the company to serve the enormous Chinese electric vehicle market more competitively. Tesla's Autopilot and Full Self-Driving software systems represent some of the most advanced applications of neural networks and computer vision in the automotive sector.

NVIDIA has emerged as the dominant supplier of graphics processing units used for training large-scale artificial intelligence models. Jensen Huang, who co-founded NVIDIA in 1993, has positioned the company as the essential infrastructure provider for the artificial intelligence revolution. NVIDIA's H100 GPU chips are in extraordinary demand from technology companies, cloud providers, and research institutions around the world. The company's market capitalization surpassed two trillion dollars in 2024, reflecting its central role in enabling modern artificial intelligence workloads.

The relationship between NVIDIA and major cloud providers including Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud has become increasingly important as demand for AI computing resources continues to grow exponentially. These cloud providers are also developing their own custom silicon chips to reduce dependency on NVIDIA and lower the cost of running AI inference workloads at scale. Amazon developed the Trainium and Inferentia chips for this purpose, while Google created the Tensor Processing Unit.

Deep learning frameworks developed by Google, including TensorFlow, and by Facebook, including PyTorch, have become the standard tools used by researchers and engineers worldwide to build and train neural networks. The open-source nature of these frameworks has accelerated progress across the entire field by allowing anyone with sufficient computing resources to experiment with state-of-the-art techniques. The collaborative culture of sharing research through platforms like arXiv has been essential to the rapid pace of advancement in artificial intelligence.

The integration of artificial intelligence into every major technology product represents both an extraordinary opportunity and a significant responsibility. Companies that successfully harness these capabilities will likely achieve substantial competitive advantages, while those that fail to adapt may struggle to remain relevant in an increasingly AI-driven economy.

Microsoft has strengthened its position in the enterprise market by providing AI services on the Azure cloud platform. Satya Nadella has described AI as the centerpiece of Microsoft's future. Jensen Huang of NVIDIA has described this wave of Artificial Intelligence as bigger than the Industrial Revolution. NVIDIA's H100 and A100 GPU chips are the most in-demand for training AI models. The lack of supply of these chips has affected the pace of global AI development. Cloud companies like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft are developing their own AI chips to reduce their dependence on NVIDIA. The use of AI is increasing rapidly in India also. The government has allocated special funds for AI research and development under the National AI Mission. The quality of AI research in Indian Institutes of Technology is becoming world class. Indian IT companies like Infosys, TCS and Wipro are leading the way in providing AI solutions to their customers. It is clear that Artificial Intelligence will impact every aspect of our lives in the coming decades. Ensuring the ethical use of this technology is the biggest challenge today.
document_texts.f4.txt: In recent years, artificial intelligence has developed into one of the most important technologies of our time. OpenAI, founded by Sam Altman and other leading researchers, set a milestone in the history of computer science with the development of GPT-4. This language model outperforms human experts in numerous academic and professional tests, which has sparked both excitement and concern in the scientific community. Google, through its subsidiary DeepMind, developed the Gemini model, which was positioned as a direct competitor to GPT-4. DeepMind was originally founded in London and was acquired by Google for approximately five hundred million dollars. The collaboration between Google and DeepMind has led to groundbreaking advances in protein research, game theory and language processing. Sundar Pichai, the CEO of Google, has called Gemini the most important project in the company's history. Microsoft has entered into a strategic partnership with OpenAI and invested ten billion dollars. This collaboration has led to the integration of GPT-4 into products such as Bing, Azure and Microsoft Office. Satya Nadella, the CEO of Microsoft, sees artificial intelligence as the most transformative technology since the invention of the Internet. The Bing integration has given Microsoft a significant market share in the search engine sector for the first time in years. NVIDIA, led by Jensen Huang, has established itself as an essential supplier to the AI ​​industry. NVIDIA's H100 chips are in such high demand worldwide that delivery times of several months are not uncommon. NVIDIA's market capitalization has surpassed the $2 trillion mark, reflecting the company's central role in the AI ​​revolution. European companies are also increasingly investing in AI infrastructure in order to survive in global competition. In Germany, the federal government has adopted a comprehensive AI strategy that includes investments in research, infrastructure and education. Companies like SAP and Siemens are integrating AI into their core products to strengthen their competitiveness. With the AI ​​Act, the European Union has created a globally unique regulatory framework for artificial intelligence that sets risk-based requirements for AI systems. The ethical implications of artificial intelligence are a central issue in German and European society. Data protection, transparency and fairness are fundamental values ​​that must be taken into account in AI development. German researchers are making significant contributions to the development of explainable and trustworthy AI systems.

Climate change represents the most urgent and devastating challenge facing humanity in the twenty-first century. The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change, commonly known as the IPCC, has issued stark warnings that global average temperatures have already risen by approximately 1.1 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels. Scientists at NASA and NOAA have confirmed through decades of careful measurement that this warming is primarily driven by the burning of fossil fuels and the resulting accumulation of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases in the atmosphere.

Greta Thunberg, the Swedish environmental activist, began her school strike for climate in August 2018 outside the Swedish parliament, inspiring millions of young people around the world to demand urgent action from their governments. The Fridays for Future movement that grew from her individual protest has organized some of the largest climate demonstrations in history. Thunberg has met with world leaders including Pope Francis, Barack Obama, and various heads of state, delivering an uncompromising message that current climate policies are dangerously inadequate.

The Paris Agreement, adopted in 2015 under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, committed participating nations to limiting global warming to well below 2 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels, with efforts to limit warming to 1.5 degrees. Despite this agreement, global carbon emissions have continued to rise in most years since its adoption. The United States withdrew from the Paris Agreement under President Donald Trump and subsequently rejoined under President Joe Biden, highlighting the political vulnerability of international climate commitments.

Amazon deforestation has accelerated alarmingly in recent years, with the Brazilian Amazon losing record areas of forest cover under policies that prioritized agricultural and mining development over environmental protection. Scientists warn that continued deforestation could push the Amazon past a tipping point beyond which large portions of the rainforest would transition to savanna, releasing enormous quantities of stored carbon and dramatically reducing biodiversity. The Amazon River basin contains approximately ten percent of all species on Earth and plays a crucial role in regulating South American and global climate patterns.

Renewable energy technology has advanced dramatically and costs have fallen to the point where solar and wind power are now the cheapest sources of new electricity generation in most parts of the world. Companies like Vestas in wind energy and First Solar and SunPower in solar energy have played leading roles in this transformation. Tesla's energy storage products and other battery technologies are enabling greater integration of renewable energy into electrical grids that previously required fossil fuel backup generation for reliability.

The consequences of inaction on climate change are catastrophic and irreversible on human timescales. Extreme weather events including hurricanes, floods, droughts, and wildfires are already becoming more frequent and severe. Coastal communities around the world face existential threats from sea level rise. The time for incremental action has passed and only transformative systemic change can prevent the worst outcomes.
document_texts.f7.txt: Climate change is one of the most serious challenges facing humanity in the twenty-first century. The United Nations Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change has issued alarming scientific reports confirming that global temperatures have risen by 1.1 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels. Major industrialized countries such as the United States of America, China, and the European Union bear the greatest historical responsibility for this rise as a result of burning fossil fuels for many decades. The Paris Climate Agreement, signed by one hundred and eighty-nine countries in 2015, aims to limit temperature rises to less than two degrees Celsius. Greta Thunberg, the young Swedish activist, launched the school strike movement for climate and inspired millions of young people around the world to demand climate justice. Thunberg met world leaders at the United Nations and in front of European parliaments, bringing a clear message that time is running out and future generations will not forgive inaction. Deforestation in the Brazilian Amazon has reached record levels of serious scientific concern. The Amazon rainforest contains ten percent of all living organisms on the planet and plays a pivotal role in regulating the global climate. Scientists warn that continued destruction could push this vital ecosystem towards a point of no return, where dense forests turn into dry savannah. Renewable energy has made amazing progress, with solar and wind energy becoming the lowest costs in human history. Companies like Vestas Wind and First Solar are leaders in this major energy transition. Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates are investing heavily in solar energy despite their historical dependence on oil and gas. Climate disasters are on the rise at an alarming rate in various parts of the world. The catastrophic floods in Pakistan in 2022 affected thirty million people and proved that the countries of the Global South suffer the most severe consequences, even though they are historically the least responsible for climate change. Climate justice requires that rich countries pay for the damage they inflict on developing countries. The future depends on political will, international cooperation and a rapid transition towards a zero-carbon economy. Every delay in taking the necessary action increases the human and economic costs and makes solutions more difficult and complex.

காலநிலை மாற்றம் இருபத்தியொன்றாம் நூற்றாண்டில் மனிதகுலம் எதிர்கொள்ளும் மிக முக்கியமான சவாலாக மாறியுள்ளது. ஐக்கிய நாடுகள் சபையின் காலநிலை மாற்றம் தொடர்பான அரசிடை குழு வெளியிட்ட அறிக்கைகள் உலக வெப்பநிலை தொழில் புரட்சிக்கு முந்தைய காலத்தை விட 1.1 டிகிரி செல்சியஸ் அதிகரித்துள்ளது என்பதை உறுதிப்படுத்துகின்றன. நாசா மற்றும் நோவா ஆய்வு நிறுவனங்கள் இந்த வெப்பமயமாதலுக்கு எரிபொருள் எரிப்பே முக்கிய காரணம் என்று நிரூபித்துள்ளன.

சுவீடிஷ் இளம் சுற்றுச்சூழல் ஆர்வலர் கிரேட்டா தன்பெர்க் 2018 ஆகஸ்ட் மாதம் சுவீடன் பாராளுமன்றத்திற்கு வெளியே பள்ளிப் புரட்டால் தொடங்கினார். இந்த ஒற்றை நபர் போராட்டம் உலகம் முழுவதும் பல மில்லியன் இளைஞர்களை ஈர்த்தது. தன்பெர்க் பாப் பிரான்சிஸ், பராக் ஒபாமா மற்றும் பல உலகத் தலைவர்களைச் சந்தித்து காலநிலை நடவடிக்கைகள் போதுமானதாக இல்லை என்று கடுமையாக எச்சரித்தார். பாரிஸ் ஒப்பந்தம் 2015 இல் கையெழுத்தானது இருந்தும் உலக கார்பன் வெளியீடுகள் தொடர்ந்து அதிகரிக்கின்றன.

அமேசான் காடுகள் அழிவு மிகவும் கவலைப்பட வேண்டிய நிலையில் உள்ளது. பிரேசிலின் அமேசான் பகுதியில் கோடிக்கணக்கான மரங்கள் வெட்டப்படுகின்றன. விஞ்ஞானிகள் எச்சரிக்கின்றனர் இந்த காடுகள் ஒரு திரும்பும் புள்ளியை தாண்டினால் சவன்னாவாக மாறி மிகப்பெரிய அளவிலான கார்பனை வெளியிடும். அமேசான் நதி படுகை பூமியிலுள்ள அனைத்து உயிரினங்களில் பத்து சதவீதத்தை உள்ளடக்கியது என்பது இதன் முக்கியத்துவத்தை காட்டுகிறது.

புதுப்பிக்கத்தக்க ஆற்றல் தொழில்நுட்பம் வியத்தகு முன்னேற்றம் கண்டுள்ளது. சூரிய சக்தி மற்றும் காற்றாலை மின்சாரம் இப்போது உலகின் பெரும்பாலான இடங்களில் மலிவான மின்சார ஆதாரங்களாக மாறியுள்ளன. வெஸ்டாஸ் நிறுவனம் காற்றாலை ஆற்றலிலும் ஃபர்ஸ்ட் சோலார் நிறுவனம் சூரிய ஆற்றலிலும் முன்னோடியாக செயல்படுகின்றன. டெஸ்லா நிறுவனம் மின்கார உற்பத்தியுடன் ஆற்றல் சேமிப்பு தீர்வுகளையும் வழங்குகிறது.

தமிழ்நாட்டில் காலநிலை மாற்றத்தின் விளைவுகள் மிகவும் தெளிவாக தெரிகின்றன. வடகிழக்கு பருவமழை ஒழுங்கற்றதாகிவிட்டது மற்றும் வெப்பமான கோடைக்காலங்கள் அதிகமாகியுள்ளன. சென்னை நகரம் கடல் மட்ட உயர்வினால் முன்னெப்போதும் இல்லாத வெள்ள ஆபத்தை எதிர்கொள்கிறது. விவசாயிகள் மாறும் மழைநீர் வடிவங்களால் கடுமையான நெருக்கடியில் உள்ளனர்.

உலகெங்கும் உள்ள இளைஞர்கள் காலநிலை நடவடிக்கைக்கு குரல் கொடுக்கின்றனர். அரசியல் இச்சாசக்தியும் சர்வதேச ஒத்துழைப்பும் மட்டுமே இந்த நெருக்கடியை தீர்க்க முடியும்.

document_texts.f10.txt:
The COVID-19 pandemic, caused by the SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus, represents the most significant global health crisis since the influenza pandemic of 1918. The World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a public health emergency of international concern in January 2020 and a pandemic in March 2020. The virus first emerged in Wuhan, China in late 2019 and rapidly spread to every country on Earth, killing millions of people and causing unprecedented economic disruption. The pandemic exposed deep vulnerabilities in global health systems and supply chains that had been building for decades.

Pfizer and BioNTech developed the first mRNA vaccine against COVID-19, with Pfizer's CEO Albert Bourla announcing remarkable efficacy results in November 2020. The vaccine was authorized for emergency use by regulatory agencies including the FDA in the United States and the EMA in Europe within months of the pandemic being declared. Moderna, another biotechnology company, developed a second highly effective mRNA vaccine using similar technology. The speed at which these vaccines were developed was unprecedented in the history of medicine, made possible by decades of prior research into mRNA technology and enormous government investment.

The development and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines represented one of the greatest achievements in the history of public health. However, vaccine inequity became a major ethical and practical problem, with wealthy nations hoarding vaccine supplies while low-income countries struggled to vaccinate their populations. The COVAX initiative, led by the WHO, Gavi the Vaccine Alliance, and the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations, worked to improve equitable distribution but faced enormous logistical and funding challenges.

Anthony Fauci, who served as director of the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases for decades, became the most prominent public health official in the United States during the pandemic. Fauci provided clear scientific guidance during periods of enormous uncertainty and faced intense political pressure that he consistently resisted in favor of evidence-based recommendations. His relationship with the Trump administration became strained as the administration sometimes contradicted scientific consensus on issues including mask wearing and treatment options.

Long COVID, a condition in which patients experience persistent symptoms for months or years after acute infection, emerged as a significant public health concern affecting millions of people worldwide. Symptoms include severe fatigue, brain fog, breathing difficulties, and cardiovascular problems that can be debilitating. Research institutions including the National Institutes of Health launched major studies to understand the mechanisms of long COVID and develop effective treatments. The condition highlighted the importance of taking viral infections seriously even when initial symptoms appear mild.

The pandemic accelerated the development of digital health technologies including telemedicine platforms, remote patient monitoring systems, and AI-powered diagnostic tools. Companies like Teladoc, Amwell, and Epic Systems saw dramatic increases in adoption of their platforms. The integration of these technologies into mainstream healthcare has permanently changed how medicine is practiced in many countries.
document_texts.f11.txt: The COVID-19 pandemic has put global health systems to an unprecedented test. The novel coronavirus SARS-CoV-2 spread from Wuhan in China to all countries in the world within a few months and claimed millions of lives. The World Health Organization, under its Director General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, declared a global health emergency in March 2020. Older people and people with previous illnesses such as diabetes, heart disease and respiratory diseases were particularly affected. Pfizer and BioNTech developed the world's first mRNA vaccine against COVID-19 in record time. Albert Bourla, the CEO of Pfizer, announced the exceptionally high effectiveness of the vaccine in November 2020. BioNTech, a German biotechnology company, had been researching mRNA technology for many years and was now able to use this expertise in the crisis. The European Medicines Agency and the FDA in the US granted emergency approval within a few months. Moderna also developed an mRNA vaccine with comparable effectiveness. The rapid development of both vaccines was only possible through decades of basic research and massive government investments. Germany invested significant resources in vaccine development through BioNTech, contributing to one of the most significant medical achievements in history. The federal government under Chancellor Angela Merkel coordinated European vaccine procurement and national vaccination campaigns. Long COVID as a secondary disease affects millions of people worldwide and presents health systems with new challenges. Those affected suffer from persistent fatigue, problems concentrating and shortness of breath, which can persist for months or years after the acute infection. German research institutions including the Charité in Berlin and the German Center for Infection Research are working intensively on research into this disease. The social and economic costs of Long COVID are enormous. The pandemic has significantly accelerated the digitalization of the German healthcare system. Telemedicine platforms boomed and were used by millions of patients for the first time. Companies such as Techniker Krankenkasse and Gematik are working to sustainably modernize the digital infrastructure of the healthcare system. Artificial intelligence is increasingly being used for the early detection of diseases and the development of new drugs. The lessons from the pandemic must be incorporated into a better global health architecture. Pandemic prevention, vaccine equity and the strengthening of international health institutions are the tasks of the coming decades.

Pfizer CEO Albert Bourla announced the extraordinary effectiveness of the vaccine in November 2020. The US FDA and Europe's EMA granted emergency use authorization within a few months. The Covid pandemic in India exposed serious shortcomings in health services. Lack of oxygen, non-availability of beds in hospitals and black marketing of medicines devastated thousands of families. The lessons learned from this pandemic will require us to build a strong global health framework. It is our collective responsibility to prepare for future pandemics and ensure equitable access to health.
document_texts.f14.txt:
The FIFA World Cup 2022 held in Qatar was one of the most dramatic and controversial tournaments in the history of international football. Lionel Messi, widely considered the greatest footballer of his generation, finally led Argentina to World Cup glory in a final against France that many observers described as the greatest World Cup final ever played. The match ended 3-3 after extra time with Messi scoring twice, before Argentina won on penalties. For Messi, who had long lived in the shadow of his predecessor Diego Maradona in terms of World Cup achievement, the victory was a culmination of decades of dedication and brilliance.

Kylian Mbappe of France delivered one of the most astonishing individual performances in World Cup final history, scoring a hat-trick including two goals in rapid succession that drew France level from 2-0 down. Despite his heroics France could not prevent Argentina from winning their third World Cup title. Mbappe, who was just twenty-three years old at the time of the final, plays for Paris Saint-Germain in Ligue 1 and is widely expected to become the defining footballer of the next decade. Real Madrid has been linked with a record-breaking transfer for Mbappe for several years.

The esports industry has grown into a multibillion-dollar global phenomenon with professional tournaments attracting audiences that rival traditional sports. Riot Games developed League of Legends, which has become one of the most watched esports globally with major tournaments filling football stadiums across Asia and North America. Valve Corporation's Dota 2 features The International tournament with prize pools that have reached over forty million dollars, funded through a community crowdfunding model that has become a template for the industry.

Fortnite, developed by Epic Games, transformed the gaming industry with its free-to-play battle royale model and became a cultural phenomenon that transcended gaming. At its peak Fortnite had over three hundred and fifty million registered accounts and hosted virtual concerts featuring artists including Travis Scott and Ariana Grande that drew tens of millions of concurrent viewers. Epic Games founder Tim Sweeney has been a vocal critic of Apple's App Store policies and led a legal battle against Apple that became a landmark case in digital marketplace regulation.

The NBA continues to be one of the most globally popular sports leagues in the world. LeBron James of the Los Angeles Lakers has become the all-time leading scorer in NBA history, surpassing the record previously held by Kareem Abdul-Jabbar. Stephen Curry of the Golden State Warriors revolutionized basketball with his extraordinary three-point shooting ability, inspiring a generation of players worldwide to develop long-range shooting skills. The NBA's partnership with the Chinese market has been complicated by political tensions but remains strategically important.

The intersection of technology and sports continues to deepen through applications including advanced analytics, computer vision-based performance tracking, virtual reality training environments, and AI-powered injury prevention systems. Major League Baseball adopted the automated ball-strike system in minor league games to improve officiating consistency and is considering expanding it to the major leagues.
